tracks.meaning: a disturbance that travels through a medium, transferring energy without permanently displacing the medium itself
a moving ridge or swell on the surface of water, typically caused by wind or tidal forces
a moving system of disturbed atmospheric conditions, such as a heat wave or cold wave
to move one's hand or an object back and forth or up and down in a signal or greeting
to signal or acknowledge someone by moving one's hand or an object in a sweeping motion
a gentle undulating curve or series of curves in hair
a ridge or series of ridges formed on a surface, such as sand or snow, by wind or water
a sudden widespread trend, movement, or phenomenon affecting a large group of people
a physical property of sound characterized by frequency that determines pitch
a graphical representation of audio or signal data shown as an oscillating line
a successive group or assault of military units or aircraft attacking in sequence
a period or cycle of activity or conditions that rises and falls in intensity
